scenarios.initial_state: An immutable accepted execution container is explicitly classified as inactive history while separate current work remains active.
scenarios.trigger: The harness selects validation authority or attempts to load the historical container as executable input.
scenarios.expected_decision: Select only the pinned frozen contract for the historical container, exclude it from active reconciliation, and reject execution of that container.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Validating history under the current contract, silently treating it as active, or rewriting it to the newest shape.
invariants: Historical documents are not current execution authority.
Accepted history is not rewritten.
Compatibility adapters translate inputs and do not rewrite history.

scenarios.initial_state: A record pair explicitly selects the current validation contract and contains a deterministic semantic error.
scenarios.trigger: Validation under the selected contract fails.
scenarios.expected_decision: Return a blocking validation result bound to the selected version and do not attempt any older validator.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Accepting the records because an older or weaker contract happens to pass.
invariants: Routing cannot bypass validator failures.
Active work cannot switch contract version mid-node without explicit authority.

scenarios.source_failure_class: insufficient Git ancestry for baseline derivation
scenarios.initial_state: The checkout is shallow and the authoritative comparison base is not reachable locally.
scenarios.trigger: Candidate freezing or continuity derives changed surface after merge-base lookup fails.
scenarios.expected_decision: Return HANDOFF_READY with an observable baseline-history-unavailable reason and do not mark the candidate eligible.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Treating the oldest locally reachable shallow commit as the proven repository baseline and under-reporting changed files.
invariants: Full verification runs only for a frozen eligible candidate.
A host that cannot prove a boundary reports the limitation honestly.
scenarios.notes: Observed implementation risk and requested candidate; no verified historical incident or direct regression was found.

scenarios.initial_state: Repository root and target are presented through platform-specific case, separator, short-name, volume, or filesystem aliases.
scenarios.trigger: Containment, ownership, or changed-path admission compares the two paths.
scenarios.expected_decision: Canonicalize aliases to one project-relative identity when they resolve to the same in-root object and reject sibling, other-volume, traversal, or symlink escape.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Treating aliases of one object as independent owners or accepting an escaped target because its lexical prefix matches the root.
invariants: Parallelism requires ownership independence.
Changed paths stay within authorized boundaries.
Cross-platform capability is preserved.
scenarios.notes: Windows and POSIX evidence exists; a macOS-specific alias case remains an evaluation gap.

scenarios.initial_state: The worktree contains staged, unstaged, and non-ignored untracked changes before candidate freeze.
scenarios.trigger: The controller requests freeze under clean-head mode or explicitly authorized working-tree mode.
scenarios.expected_decision: Reject clean-head freeze; allow working-tree freeze only with explicit digest-bound snapshot authority that binds every dirty layer and detects drift.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Freezing an unbound dirty tree, ignoring staged or untracked state, or treating snapshot authority as a permission upgrade.
invariants: Full verification runs only for a frozen eligible candidate.
A node cannot grant itself authority.
Changed dependencies invalidate evidence.

scenarios.initial_state: A managed command has a bounded deadline and may spawn descendants or race with heartbeat completion.
scenarios.trigger: The deadline expires, including when the child reports success only after the deadline.
scenarios.expected_decision: Classify TIMEOUT, terminate and reap the owned process tree, report cleanup and containment, and refuse success when cleanup cannot be proven.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Reporting success after deadline, claiming zero orphans without a completed check, or hiding failed cleanup.
invariants: Host enforcement limitations are reported honestly.
Failure is diagnosed at the lowest failing layer.
scenarios.notes: Direct integration coverage and targeted historical timeout provenance exist.

scenarios.source_failure_class: duplicate full review or final verification
scenarios.initial_state: A complete batched review and final verification already exist for an unchanged scope and candidate fingerprint.
scenarios.trigger: A controller proposes another full reviewer wave or full-suite attempt only to increase perceived confidence.
scenarios.expected_decision: Deny the duplicate broad run, preserve existing evidence and counters, and continue with the recorded decision or return a resumable handoff.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Launching a second broad review, overwriting the first report, or counting repetition as independent confidence.
invariants: Unchanged evidence is reused.
Use the minimum decisive verification.
Full verification runs once per eligible candidate and kind.
scenarios.notes: Executable coverage is strongest for full-suite duplication; broad reviewer-wave enforcement remains mostly documentary.

scenarios.initial_state: Initial review is complete and the correction changes only a ledger, evidence record, status, closeout, or pointer without semantic or authority change.
scenarios.trigger: Corrective closure selects the required re-review scope.
scenarios.expected_decision: Use strict deterministic closure when its pre-authored predicate passes; otherwise run only targeted review of the affected record and direct regressions.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Starting another full review, recording deterministic closure as a passed re-review, or accepting fixer self-report as the verdict.
invariants: Review remains independent from implementation.
Unchanged evidence is reused.
Use the minimum decisive verification.
scenarios.notes: Direct document-contract and convergence evidence exists.

scenarios.initial_state: An unaccepted writable candidate file differs only by an extra EOF blank line, missing final newline, or trailing horizontal whitespace.
scenarios.trigger: Candidate preflight classifies the byte difference.
scenarios.expected_decision: Return AUTO_NORMALIZATION_CORRECTIVE for the exact authorized file, preserve the semantic digest, and require only diff, digest, focused checks, and targeted review.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Stopping for product authority solely for ordinary whitespace, silently changing semantics, or auto-fixing frozen, generated, signed, or checksum-bound content.
invariants: Use the minimum decisive verification.
Historical documents are not rewritten.
A bounded correction cannot expand writable scope.
scenarios.notes: Direct tests and historical formatting-only corrections exist.

scenarios.initial_state: A specialist capability is optional under active authority and a safe native workflow remains available.
scenarios.trigger: Capability discovery reports that the specialist capability is absent.
scenarios.expected_decision: Record unavailable capability metadata and continue through the authorized native fallback without installation or a product-failure verdict.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Marking the project BLOCKED, inventing a gate, silently installing tooling, or treating discovery metadata as authority.
invariants: Optional findings do not become active work without authority.
A node cannot grant itself authority.
Capability absence does not remove an accepted native outcome.
scenarios.notes: Normative coverage is strong; a dedicated end-to-end fallback test is absent.

scenarios.initial_state: The selected host cannot provide a required containment level before target launch.
scenarios.expected_decision: Return CAPABILITY_LIMITATION with target-not-started evidence and do not classify the product or tests as failed.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Reporting PASS, NEEDS_CORRECTION, or product failure, or launching the target despite the unmet boundary.
invariants: A host reports unenforceable boundaries honestly.
Routing cannot bypass safety boundaries.
Failure is corrected at the lowest failing layer.

scenarios.initial_state: The requested decision is already satisfied by valid unchanged evidence while speculative changes, reviewers, and broad tests have no authority or changed-surface link.
scenarios.trigger: The controller performs graph admission and proposed result classification.
scenarios.expected_decision: Classify speculative items as OPTIONAL or OUT_OF_SCOPE, admit no new nodes, and propose NO_ACTION_REQUIRED with inspected scope, applicable decision, and reused evidence.
scenarios.forbidden_false_green: Inventing implementation work, adding reviewers or tests with no decision value, or claiming no-action without valid evidence.
invariants: Speculative risk does not enter active work without an authority, changed-surface, evidence, or acceptance link.
A verified no-action result is valid.
A graph node must earn its coordination cost.
scenarios.notes: Blueprint evaluation candidate only; current runtime does not implement the proposed result.
